activeWindow.note: Outside this window, the monitor will not run and will not create results, incidents, or notifications.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Monitoring Command Center
dashboard.description: Health overview of monitors, open incidents, at-risk groups, and latest results — all in one place.
dashboard.noQueueIncidents: No open incidents in the queue.
dashboard.createMonitorsChart: Create monitors to populate this chart.
dashboard.runChecksMsg: Run checks to see response time trends.
dashboard.allHealthy: All enabled monitors are currently healthy.
dashboard.noOpenIncidentsMsg: Nothing is currently waiting for incident handling.
dashboard.createGroupsMsg: Create groups to see business or device-level health.
dashboard.addMonitorsMsg: Add monitors to see inventory coverage.
dashboard.populatingMsg: Recent checks will populate this ranking.
dashboard.noFailuresMsg: Latest checks are not reporting down or degraded states.

results.title: Monitor Results
results.subtitle: Monitoring
results.description: All monitor check results in one place — quickly find problem periods, mass down events, and abnormal response times.
results.noResults: No results found in this time range
results.footerHint: Time filters support daily, weekly, monthly, and custom range
results.pageSnapshot: Page 1 counts in the current filter:

alerts.title: Alert Rules
alerts.subtitle: Alerting
alerts.description: Configure alert conditions and notification channels per monitor from this central view.
alerts.deviceThresholdsDesc: For SYSTEM/SNMP monitors: CPU, RAM and Disk
alerts.modalDesc: Select monitor, threshold, and channels to notify.
alerts.noChannelsHint: No channels yet. If none selected, rule will notify all enabled channels once configured.
alerts.validationDeviceMonitor: CPU/RAM/Disk rules require a SYSTEM or SNMP monitor

channels.title: Notification Channels
channels.subtitle: Alerting
channels.description: Configure notification channels for incident open/resolved events. Supports Telegram, LINE, Email, Slack, Discord, and custom webhooks.
channels.modalDesc: Notifications will be sent through this channel when an incident occurs.
channels.tokenPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep existing token
channels.passwordPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep existing password
channels.webhookPayloadExample: Example payload that will be sent
channels.testDraftError: Failed to send test from form
channels.validationBotToken: Please enter a Telegram bot token
channels.validationChatId: Please enter a Telegram chat ID
channels.validationLineToken: Please enter a LINE channel access token
channels.validationEmailFull: Please fill in all SMTP fields

devices.title: Devices
devices.subtitle: Monitoring
devices.description: SNMP devices and system monitors for CPU, RAM, disk, and traffic counters.
devices.groupHint: Use groups to focus devices by site, tenant, or system role without mixing the whole inventory.
devices.emptyHint: Create a SYSTEM monitor to collect CPU/RAM/Disk via SNMP, or use an SNMP monitor for device identity and interface counters.
devices.noSystemMetrics: No CPU/RAM/Disk metrics for this device yet

interfaces.title: Interfaces
interfaces.subtitle: Inventory
interfaces.description: View all interfaces across inventory, error hotspots, and links that need attention.
interfaces.noisyDesc: Sorted by errors/discards first, then traffic counters.
interfaces.emptyHint: A successful SNMP or SYSTEM monitor check with interface counters is required first.
interfaces.noMatch: No interfaces match the selected filters

groups.title: Groups
groups.subtitle: Inventory
groups.description: Organize monitors and devices by branch, customer, environment, or owning team so inventory maps to real work.
groups.healthSnapshot: Snapshot from each group's latest result
groups.emptyHint: Start with a group by site, customer, or production environment.
groups.ungroupedDesc: Monitors not yet grouped, useful for finishing inventory organization.
groups.modalDesc: Group monitors and devices by the real operational context they belong to.
groups.assignMonitorsDesc: Select monitors or devices to include in this group.
groups.deleteDesc: Deleting a group does not delete monitors, but removes all member grouping.
groups.detailDescriptionFallback: View health overview, recent incidents, noisy monitors, and shortcuts to views filtered to this group.
groups.noMonitorsInGroup: No monitors in this group yet
groups.noIncidentsInGroup: No incidents in this group yet

credentials.title: Credentials
credentials.subtitle: Inventory
credentials.description: Store centralized credential inventory for SNMP, SSH, API, and username/password access so multiple monitors can reuse the same set and usage is visible in one place.
credentials.validationMetadataObject: Metadata must be a JSON object
credentials.validationCommunity: Please enter a name and community
credentials.validationPassword: Please enter a name and password
credentials.validationApiToken: Please enter a name and API token
credentials.validationPrivateKey: Please enter a name and private key
credentials.validationCloudflareClientId: Cloudflare Access credentials require a Client ID
credentials.validationCloudflareClientSecret: Please enter a name and Cloudflare Access Client Secret
credentials.validationUsername: Username / Password credentials require a username
credentials.vaultHint: Use credentials as shared secrets and see which monitors are linked to each one.
credentials.notLinked: Not linked to any monitor yet
credentials.guideDescription: Each credential type is used differently, and monitor creation only shows the relevant types.
credentials.modalDescription: Store connection details for reuse across multiple monitors.
credentials.deleteDescription: If this credential is in use, linked monitors will be unlinked automatically.
credentials.deleteUnlinkHint: After deletion, these monitors will remain but will no longer reference this credential.
credentials.guideSnmpSummary: Store an SNMP community string for SNMP and SYSTEM monitors.
credentials.guideUserPassSummary: Store a username/password pair for database monitors, HTTP basic auth, and login-based checks.
credentials.guideApiTokenSummary: Store a token or API key such as a bearer token or Portainer API key.
credentials.guideSshKeySummary: Store an SSH private key for future monitor or agent flows that require key-based access.
credentials.guideCloudflareAccessSummary: Store a Cloudflare Access service token for Portainer or other protected internal tools.
credentials.guideFieldSnmpMetadata: Metadata JSON such as version, port, vendor
credentials.guideFieldAuthMetadata: Metadata JSON such as authSource, databaseType
credentials.guideFieldTokenMetadata: Metadata JSON such as header name, scope
credentials.guideFieldSshMetadata: Metadata JSON such as port, passphrase hint
credentials.usedForDockerCloudflareAccess: Optional Cloudflare Access headers for Docker / Portainer monitor
credentials.keepExistingPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep existing value

reportsPage.title: Reports
reportsPage.subtitle: Overview
reportsPage.description: Summarize uptime, incidents, response time, and at-risk monitors for the selected time range.
reportsPage.statusMixDescription: Result distribution in the selected window
reportsPage.noChecksMessage: No results found in this report window.
reportsPage.responseTrendDescription: Latest response samples in the report window
reportsPage.noResponseMessage: Response time will appear once checks exist.
reportsPage.monitorRankingDescription: Sorted by down checks, degraded checks, and uptime
reportsPage.noMonitorMessage: No monitor checks found in this report window.
reportsPage.incidentReportDescription: Recent incidents within the report window
reportsPage.noIncidentsMessage: No incidents were found in this report window.
reportsPage.groupSummaryDescription: Report metrics grouped by monitor group
reportsPage.noGroupMessage: Create monitor groups or run checks to populate this section.

statusMap.title: Status Map
statusMap.subtitle: Overview
statusMap.description: A status map of monitors by group, helping show what is down, which service is affected, and where to investigate first.
statusMap.controlsDescription: Filter map nodes by scope, issue state, or device monitor type.
statusMap.noNodesMessage: Change the group or focus filter to show more monitors.
statusMap.noPriorityMessage: No down, degraded, or incident-backed monitors right now.
statusMap.ungroupedDescription: Monitors not assigned to a group

systemLogs.title: System Logs
systemLogs.subtitle: System
systemLogs.description: Internal event log for monitor runner, retention, notifications, and errors kept for 90 days.

scheduledReports.title: Scheduled Reports
scheduledReports.description: Send automatic monitor status summary reports at a scheduled time through notification channels.
scheduledReports.dailyDescription: A status report for every monitor with downtime windows from the last 24 hours.
scheduledReports.timeHint: The report covers the previous 24 hours from this time.
scheduledReports.sampleRecovered: OK web2 - online now, but had downtime in this period
scheduledReports.allEnabledChannels: None selected -> send to every enabled channel ({{count}} channels)
scheduledReports.noChannels: No notification channel yet. Create one from Channels first.

profile.title: My Profile
profile.roleHint: Role is assigned by Admin only

loginHistory.title: My Login History
loginHistory.description: View successful and failed logins for this account, including the recorded IP address and device.

changePassword.title: Change Password
changePassword.description: After changing your password, the system will revoke every session and ask you to log in again.
changePassword.validationSame: New password must be different from the current password
changePassword.revokeHint: Changing your password will revoke every session, including this one, and you will need to log in again.

users.title: Users
users.description: Manage system user accounts, create users, edit profiles, reset passwords, and assign permissions.
users.validationRequired: Please fill in all required fields
users.selfRoleHint: You cannot change your own role

auditLogs.title: Audit Logs
auditLogs.description: Review important system activity such as cleanup, incident reminders, and recorded actions.
auditLogs.noLogs: No audit logs match these filters

apiTokens.title: API Tokens
apiTokens.description: Create tokens for direct API access without a session. Use them in scripts, integrations, or automation.
apiTokens.copyNow: Copy this token now. It will not be shown again.
apiTokens.authHeader: Use it in the HTTP header:
apiTokens.noTokens: No API token yet. Click New Token to get started.

notificationsPage.title: All Notifications
notificationsPage.description: A center for events related to you: incidents, delivery, system, and monitor activity.

domain.title: Domain Intelligence
domain.description: Analyze registrar, DNS, nameserver, and subdomain information for a domain.
domain.legalDescriptionPrefix: This tool queries public DNS, opens TCP connections, and sends HTTP/TLS requests directly to target servers. Using it on unauthorized domains may violate
domain.legalDescriptionSuffix: and similar cybercrime laws in other countries.
domain.consentText: I confirm that the domain I will scan is mine, or that I have written authorization from the owner, and I accept full responsibility for this use.
domain.scanError: Unable to analyze this domain. Please check the domain name and try again.
domain.scanningHint: Checking passive sources, external tools, DNS records, and HTTP services
domain.noSubdomains: No subdomains found from passive sources
domain.noSubdomainsHint: External tools and DNS brute force have also been tried.
domain.noHistoryHint: Run your first domain scan, then come back here.

incidents.title: Incidents
incidents.subtitle: Monitoring
incidents.description: View down or abnormal events that need action, rather than scrolling through raw checks. Easily see which monitors started having issues and whether they're still open.
incidents.noIncidents: No incidents found in this time range
incidents.footerHint: Filter incidents by day, week, month, or any custom incident window
incidents.validationRange: Please select a complete time range
incidents.validationRangeOrder: Start time must not be later than end time

monitors.title: Monitors
monitors.subtitle: Monitoring
monitors.description: Latest status, recent downtime history, and 24h uptime for all monitors.
monitors.validationInterval: Interval must be at least 10 seconds
monitors.validationConfigObject: Config must be a JSON object
monitors.groupFilterHint: Filter monitors by group to isolate environments, sites, or service zones.
monitors.deleteDesc: This will delete the monitor along with all related check results and data.

settings.title: System Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure branding, alerting, monitor defaults, security, and data retention.

general.title: General
general.description: System name, tagline, logo text, and sidebar image logo.

alerting.title: Alerting Defaults
alerting.description: Reminder frequency while incidents remain open.

security.title: Security
security.description: Password, session, and login attempt policies enforced by the backend.

email.title: Email
email.description: SMTP for password reset codes and system email.

retention.title: Data retention
retention.description: Set data age by type and choose whether the scheduler cleans up automatically every 24 hours.

settings.logoHint: PNG, JPG, WEBP, GIF — up to 2 MB · Replaces sidebar logo text
settings.passwordComplexityHint: Enable requirements for user creation, password reset, password changes, and forgot password.
settings.requireLowercaseHint: Must include at least one a-z character
settings.requireUppercaseHint: Must include at least one A-Z character
settings.requireNumberHint: Must include at least one 0-9 digit
settings.requireSpecialHint: Must include a symbol such as ! @ # $
settings.emailConfiguredHint: The system will use this SMTP account to send password reset verification codes.
settings.editSmtpHint: Edit SMTP settings for sending password reset codes.
settings.setupSmtpHint: SMTP is not configured yet. Fill in the details below so the system can email password reset codes.
settings.autoCleanupHint: Enable this so the backend runs cleanup based on retention every 24 hours.
settings.retentionResultsDescription: Status check results and response messages.
settings.retentionMetricsDescription: CPU, RAM, disk, and network samples for historical charts.
settings.manualClearDescription: Intentionally clear selected data types now, either all data or only old data.
settings.cleanupNoMatchedData: Cleanup completed, but no matching data needed deletion.
settings.cleanupConfirm: Run cleanup using the current retention settings now?
settings.cleanupDone: Cleanup finished: deleted {{results}} results, {{metrics}} metrics, {{audit}} audit logs
settings.cleanupNoExpiredSuffix: (no data older than retention needed deletion)
settings.clearSummaryAll: Delete all history for the selected data types.
settings.clearSummaryExpired: Delete only data older than {{days}} days for the selected data types.
settings.clearSelectWarning: Select at least one data type before clearing.
settings.clearConfirm:
{{summary}}

Clearing history permanently deletes data and cannot be undone. Continue?
settings.clearDone: History cleared: deleted {{results}} results, {{metrics}} metrics, {{audit}} audit logs

metrics.label: Device metrics
metrics.description: CPU, RAM, disk, and network samples.

monitorDetail.title: Monitor Detail
monitorDetail.deviceMetricsDescription: CPU, memory, disk, and network counters for devices/servers.
monitorDetail.availabilityMapDescription: View status by day and hour to spot downtime or degraded periods.
monitorDetail.noAvailability: Not enough data for the availability map yet
monitorDetail.noChartData: No data for this chart yet
monitorDetail.linkedCredentialHint: At runtime, the system uses the latest value from this credential, even if the config below still contains filled values.
monitorDetail.alertRulesDescription: Set conditions and notification channels for this monitor.
monitorDetail.changeCredentialHint: Change the shared credential here, or choose No linked credential to detach it.
monitorDetail.noSharedCredentialNeeded: This monitor type/config does not use shared credentials
monitorDetail.noCompatibleCredentials: No compatible credentials yet. Create one on /credentials, then come back to select it.
monitorDetail.deviceAlertThresholdsHint: Alert when a value crosses the threshold, then alert again when it returns to normal.
monitorDetail.validationRange: Please select a complete time range
monitorDetail.validationRangeOrder: Start time must not be later than end time
monitorDetail.validationInterval: Interval must be at least 10 seconds
monitorDetail.validationConfigObject: Config must be a JSON object
monitorDetail.validationMetricUnsupported: This metric is not supported for this monitor

newMonitor.title: Add Monitor
newMonitor.subtitle: Monitoring
newMonitor.description: Create a new check target. The configuration is stored as JSON and sent directly to the API.
newMonitor.noCompatibleCredentials: No compatible credentials yet. Create one on /credentials, then come back to select it.
newMonitor.credentialAutofillHint: When selected, related fields are filled automatically and the monitor is linked to this credential.
newMonitor.cloudflareAccessOptionalHint: Optional. Use this only when Portainer is behind Cloudflare Access.
newMonitor.cloudflareAccessSelectedHint: Cloudflare Access headers will be sent with Portainer API requests.
newMonitor.expectedBodyPlaceholder: Text that must exist in the response body, such as ok or healthy
newMonitor.latencyPlaceholder: DEGRADED if response is slower than this, e.g. 2000
newMonitor.customOidsPlaceholder: 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.5.0, 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0 — leave blank to use sysName, sysDescr, sysUpTime
newMonitor.containerPlaceholder: e.g. nginx or abc123def — blank with no Stack ID checks endpoint overview

typeDescriptions.TCP: Check if a host port is reachable
typeDescriptions.HTTP: Validate URL response and status code
typeDescriptions.TLS_CERT: Check certificate expiry and warn before expiration
typeDescriptions.DNS: Resolve DNS records and optionally match an expected value
typeDescriptions.SNMP: Query SNMP agent on network devices — routers, switches, firewalls
typeDescriptions.SYSTEM: Monitor CPU, RAM and disk usage via SNMP — Linux servers with snmpd

requiredHints.HTTP: Required: url. Optional: auth, body/header check, latency threshold
requiredHints.DNS: Required: host. Optional: record type, expected value, DNS server
requiredHints.SNMP: Required: host. Optional: community, version, port, custom OIDs
requiredHints.SYSTEM: Required: host. Optional: community, version, port
requiredHints.DOCKER: Required: portainerUrl, apiKey or API token credential, endpointId. Optional: Cloudflare Access credential
requiredHints.DATABASE: Required: database type, host, port. SQLite uses file path. MongoDB can use URI or authSource.
